critical_issues.issue: Two independent section parser implementations create semantic drift and inconsistent section boundaries.
critical_issues.impact: Different extractors run on different section segmentation logic, so the same paper can produce incompatible outputs and irreproducible metric extraction.
critical_issues.fix: Unify on a single section parser module with one canonical contract and regression tests for heading variants.

critical_issues.issue: RegexExtractor extracts OA/F1/IoU/Kappa from full concatenated text, not results section.
critical_issues.impact: Metrics can be pulled from Introduction/Related Work/Table captions and attributed as paper results.
critical_issues.fix: Scope metric extraction to parsed Results section only and persist section provenance for each metric.

critical_issues.issue: SectionExtractor merges regex fallback outputs after LLM outputs, including metrics and methods.
critical_issues.impact: A stricter section-based extraction can be silently overwritten/filled with weaker global regex signals.
critical_issues.fix: Use provenance-aware merge policy: never downgrade section-scoped fields with global fallback values.

critical_issues.issue: Confidence scores are heuristic completeness scores, not calibrated correctness probabilities.
critical_issues.impact: High confidence can be assigned to wrong rows, misleading downstream filtering and publication quality claims.
critical_issues.fix: Calibrate confidence against labeled validation data, separate completeness from correctness, and report calibration error.

critical_issues.issue: Validation layer score threshold (min_score=2) can preserve low-integrity rows with only title+satellite.
critical_issues.impact: Garbage rows survive filtering and pollute final dataset.
critical_issues.fix: Introduce hard validation gates for metric provenance, section quality, and mandatory field combinations per study type.

critical_issues.issue: SectionExtractor sets constant confidence=0.90 regardless of extraction quality.
critical_issues.impact: Confidence has no ranking meaning; weak and strong rows are indistinguishable.
critical_issues.fix: Replace constant with feature-based calibrated score and confidence intervals.

critical_issues.issue: LLM call error handling swallows all exceptions and returns empty result objects.
critical_issues.impact: Silent degradation to fallback behavior makes failures hard to detect and audit.
critical_issues.fix: Emit structured failure events, per-document status codes, and fail-fast option for quality-critical runs.

critical_issues.issue: Country detection uses keyword-first heuristics and post-hoc force-fill from full text.
critical_issues.impact: Region/country fields can be noisy (e.g., mentions in references or prior work sections).
critical_issues.fix: Constrain geoparsing by section and context windows; require study-area evidence snippets.

critical_issues.issue: Pipeline scalability relies on full-context reconstruction for section extraction and multiple per-paper LLM calls.
critical_issues.impact: Throughput degrades for 300+ PDFs; LLM latency dominates and can stall batch completion.
critical_issues.fix: Add async job queue, per-stage caching, and bounded parallelism with retry/backoff policies.

critical_issues.issue: No explicit dataset-level QA loop (gold set comparison, drift detection, or extraction regression suite).
critical_issues.impact: Scientific validity can regress unnoticed across code changes and corpus shifts.
critical_issues.fix: Create benchmark corpus with expected outputs and run mandatory precision/recall checks in CI.

failure_modes.scenario: Paper reports baseline metrics in Introduction and no clear Results heading.
failure_modes.what_breaks: RegexExtractor still extracts metrics from full text and marks metrics_reported=true.
failure_modes.why: Metric regex is global-scope in RegexExtractor and not section-constrained.

failure_modes.scenario: LLM endpoint is unstable for part of a batch.
failure_modes.what_breaks: Subset of papers silently downgrade to regex fallback with no hard failure signal.
failure_modes.why: SectionExtractor catches broad Exception and returns empty result for merge/fallback path.

failure_modes.scenario: Heading style uses uncommon variants (e.g., 'Experimental Validation and Analysis').
failure_modes.what_breaks: Results section is missed; metrics become null or taken from weak fallback.
failure_modes.why: Heading pattern coverage is finite and differs between the two parser modules.

failure_modes.scenario: Methods mention many model names in survey context.
failure_modes.what_breaks: Method list over-reports techniques as if used by the paper.
failure_modes.why: Keyword-trigger method extraction lacks subject-action disambiguation and citation context filtering.

failure_modes.scenario: Geographic names appear only in references or related work examples.
failure_modes.why: Force-country detection scans broad text without study-area attribution constraints.

failure_modes.scenario: Large corpus with long documents and section extractor enabled.
failure_modes.what_breaks: Runtime spikes and operational timeout risk increases sharply.
failure_modes.why: Full-context retrieval + 3 LLM calls per document creates multiplicative latency.

improvements.action: Unify section parsing into one module and add fixture-based tests for 50+ heading patterns.
improvements.expected_gain: Removes hidden coupling and improves reproducibility across extractor backends.

improvements.action: Attach provenance to every extracted field (section, sentence span, rule/model source).
improvements.expected_gain: Enables auditable scientific claims and strict downstream filtering.

improvements.action: Constrain all metric extraction to validated Results spans and enforce section-level hard rules.
improvements.expected_gain: Major reduction in false metric attribution and reviewer-facing errors.

improvements.action: Replace heuristic confidence with calibrated quality model on labeled corpus.
improvements.expected_gain: Confidence becomes meaningful for triage and publication-grade filtering.

improvements.action: Add benchmark suite (gold annotations) with per-field precision/recall and fail thresholds in CI.
improvements.expected_gain: Prevents silent quality drift and enforces scientific rigor over time.

improvements.action: Refactor SectionExtractor merge strategy to avoid fallback overriding stricter section outputs.
improvements.expected_gain: Reduces hidden contamination from weaker extraction layers.

improvements.action: Implement async processing and retry budget for LLM stage with robust observability.
improvements.expected_gain: Improves throughput and fault tolerance at 300+ PDF scale.
Filters structurally inconsistent rows before final export.

improvements.action: Normalize method extraction with context-aware patterns (used/applied vs mentioned/cited).
improvements.expected_gain: Improves methodological precision for meta-analysis tasks.

reviewer_comment: This pipeline is not acceptable for publication-grade scientific dataset generation in its current form. The core extraction claims are undercut by non-calibrated confidence, inconsistent section parsing, and fallback pathways that can silently reintroduce weak global regex evidence into ostensibly section-constrained outputs. A Q1 reviewer would reject the dataset methodology due to insufficient provenance, weak validation design, and high risk of false metric attribution.
